title: centaurea × brassoiana
intro: Centaurea × brassoiana, also known as Brasso's cornflower, is a hybrid plant species in the family Asteraceae, resulting from the cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea scabiosa. It is valued for its potential medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory problems. Centaurea × brassoiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat colds, fever, and other infections.
